# Tool — create_entity_type

> Auto-generated by `spec/tools/gen_spec.py`. Do not hand-edit.

## Synopsis

Canon: §Entity — scaffolds an EntityType declaration into the active domain's graph via apply_proposal.

## Module docstring

Canon: §Entity — scaffolds an EntityType declaration into the active domain's graph via apply_proposal.

WHY: Declaring a new EntityType by hand requires constructing a valid ProposedEntityType
JSON, passing validation (kebab-case slug, exactly one initial state, valid transition
endpoints, known field kinds), and calling apply_proposal. A single typo produces a
confusing error deep in the apply pipeline. This tool parses and validates the high-level
CLI form, constructs the JSON, and delegates to apply_proposal as a subprocess — keeping
the workflow consistent with the manual apply path and testable end-to-end.

R-prefer-tool-over-hand: scaffolding belongs here because the EntityType shape must
satisfy structural contracts (lifecycle well-formedness, ENTITY_FIELD_KINDS, etc.).

Exit codes:
  0 — success (EntityType landed, docs regenerated, tests green).
  1 — failure (validation error, duplicate slug, or apply_proposal failed).

## CLI usage

```
usage: create_entity_type.py [-h] --description DESCRIPTION --states STATES
                             --transitions TRANSITIONS [--fields FIELDS]
                             [--cyclic]
                             slug

Scaffold an EntityType declaration into the active domain's graph via
apply_proposal.

positional arguments:
  slug                  Kebab-case entity type slug (e.g. 'customer',
                        'order'). Lowercase letters, digits, hyphens only,
                        starting with a letter.

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --description DESCRIPTION
                        One-line description of the entity type.
  --states STATES       Comma-separated 'NAME:kind' pairs. kind ∈
                        {initial,normal,terminal,quiescent}. Exactly one must
                        be 'initial'. Example:
                        'ACTIVE:initial,SUSPENDED:normal,CLOSED:quiescent'
  --transitions TRANSITIONS
                        Comma-separated 'event:src->dst' triples. src and dst
                        must be declared state names. Example: 'suspend:ACTIVE
                        ->SUSPENDED,close:ACTIVE->CLOSED,reopen:SUSPENDED-
                        >ACTIVE'
  --fields FIELDS       Comma-separated 'name:kind[:required[:ref_target]]'
                        tuples. kind ∈ {string,number,enum,reference,state}.
                        Append ':required' to mark the field as required.
                        Example: 'email:string:required,tier:enum,owner:refere
                        nce:stakeholder'
  --cyclic              Mark the entity lifecycle as cyclic (states can loop
                        back).
```
